---
title: "Lost password, create new certificate using prime p q etc"
date: 2011-09-16
forum: Security
---

### Post by Xeli on 2011-09-16
Hello,

I've used a keystore to sign certain files/jars but I've lost the password of this keystore.

Using a small java program I managed to get some information regarding certificate's private key it looks like this:
```

Sun RSA private CRT key, 1024 bits
  modulus:          
  public exponent:  12345
  private exponent: 
  prime p:          1271451269743902951182796024262415351005734087028423214310357769572992137775910057017951348032123451235658647669859568084286083306531963654894930559942837677
  prime q:          72262688572019373025656009520324013212454311645252149900648073708556825571138858093896729086168865622392730208372492582489007294621072663715630778425187107
  prime exponent p: 757104930066310827012353553669541771411550313211244590855870664793960933068175191304990092358236852040189092140729121232441412421580848968007732222271658323233
  prime exponent q: 185792194094713892226117118631232142141181018262694699158121762058714759334587693818131470160140103230013213123124123131536548285748654544980165011880439730479948321123
  crt coefficient:  3213123123213213123121231235629465221891945960520396989279313215226786600519182323495058820323232332232328145224896982104280336689192773590734838671828614351241248354634307221

```
Obviously the numbers used here aren't the actual numbers of the certificate.

Is it possible to create a new certificate using this information?

I'd greatly appreciate the help

Xeli

---

### Post by nitrogensixteen on 2011-09-17
google "Sun RSA private CRT key, 1024 bits", the first two results appear to present solutions to generating java KeyPair objects from these strings. the second link has code.

---
